title: ThreadsView — paginated list with dismiss and restore
description: Replace the stub in AggregatorApp/Threads/ThreadsView.swift with the full implementation. State: threads: [Thread], nextCursor: String?, sort: ThreadSort (default .importance), showDismissed: Bool (default false), plus a phase enum (loading/loaded/error). When !credentialsStore.isConfigured: show ContentUnavailableView pointing to Settings (same pattern as SourcesView). On appear and on sort/showDismissed change: fetch the first page (cursor nil), replacing the list. Infinite scroll: when the last row appears and nextCursor != nil, fetch the next page and append; guard against concurrent in-flight page loads. Pull-to-refresh via .refreshable reloads page 1. Toolbar: Menu or Picker to switch sort between importance and recent; toggle item to flip showDismissed. Rows are ThreadCardView; tapping navigates via .navigationDestination to ThreadPagerView(threads: threads, startIndex: tappedIndex). Swipe actions: when not showing dismissed, trailing Dismiss action (calls dismissThread, removes row optimistically, reinserts on failure); when showing dismissed, Restore action (calls restoreThread). .navigationTitle('Threads'). No hardcoded hex colors; Liquid Glass treatment (GlassEffectContainer, .listRowBackground(Color.clear)) consistent with SourcesView.
acceptance_criteria: Not-configured state shows ContentUnavailableView pointing to Settings when credentials are missing
Thread list loads on appear from GET /threads with default sort importance
Changing sort or showDismissed reloads the first page in the new order
Infinite scroll appends the next page when the last row is visible and nextCursor is non-nil; no duplicate rows appear
Pull-to-refresh reloads page 1 and replaces the list
Dismiss swipe action removes the thread optimistically and calls dismissThread; row is reinserted on failure
Restore swipe action is shown only when showDismissed is true and calls restoreThread
Tapping a row navigates to ThreadPagerView(threads: threads, startIndex: tappedIndex)
Loading, empty, and error states are handled
.navigationTitle is Threads
No hardcoded hex colors; Liquid Glass treatment matches SourcesView

handoff_summary.completed: Implemented ThreadsView.swift in full: LoadPhase enum (loading/loaded/error), state vars (threads, nextCursor, sort, showDismissed, isFetchingNextPage), ContentUnavailableView for unconfigured state, GlassEffectContainer+List with NavigationLink per row, toolbar Menu with Picker (sort) and Toggle (showDismissed), .navigationDestination(for: Int.self) routing to ThreadPagerView, swipe actions (Dismiss when !showDismissed / Restore when showDismissed) with optimistic removal and reinsertion on API failure, .onAppear-based infinite scroll trigger on last row, .refreshable pulling first page without spinner, onChange handlers for sort and showDismissed.
handoff_summary.risks: Swipe-action index capture: the index is captured at ForEach evaluation time; if the list mutates between user swipe and async Task execution, the index could be stale. The `guard index < threads.count` check prevents out-of-bounds crashes, but could skip the wrong row in a high-mutation scenario. Acceptable for personal-use scope.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester agent should verify all acceptance criteria: not-configured state, first-page load, sort/showDismissed filter changes, infinite scroll pagination, pull-to-refresh, dismiss (optimistic removal + rollback), restore, navigation to ThreadPagerView, and Liquid Glass styling.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: ThreadPagerView and ThreadCardView are read-only dependencies; no conflict risk. APIClient.getThreads, dismissThread, restoreThread used as-is.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: 1. LoadPhase enum (file-private) for clear state machine over nullable optionals — avoids the two-nullable ambiguity that SourcesView uses (`sources: [Source]?`) and makes the loading/error/loaded triple explicit. 2. `loadFirstPage(showSpinner:)` with a Bool parameter — pull-to-refresh passes `showSpinner: false` to keep the existing list visible during refresh, matching standard iOS UX. Sort/filter changes pass the default `true` to show a spinner since the result set changes completely. 3. `isFetchingNextPage` Bool flag (not a Task handle) — simple and sufficient; cancellation not needed since later writes overwrite earlier ones and the guard prevents duplicate concurrent fetches. 4. `NavigationLink(value: Int)` with `.navigationDestination(for: Int.self)` — type-safe navigation matching the SourcesView NavigationStack pattern; the destination closure captures `threads` at render time which is the correct array to pass to ThreadPagerView. 5. Restore button uses `.tint(.accentColor)` — semantic color, no hardcoded hex, not `.destructive` since restore is a safe action.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Test all acceptance criteria: (1) Not-configured — mock CredentialsStore.isConfigured = false → ContentUnavailableView shows with Settings copy. (2) First-page load on appear — mock getThreads returns page-1 data → threads list rendered. (3) Sort change — change Picker selection → new getThreads call with updated sort param, list replaced. (4) showDismissed toggle — Toggle flip → new getThreads call with showDismissed=true, list replaced. (5) Infinite scroll — mock nextCursor non-nil, trigger last-row onAppear → getThreads called with cursor param, items appended (no duplicates). (6) Concurrent guard — second last-row onAppear while isFetchingNextPage=true → only one in-flight request. (7) Pull-to-refresh — .refreshable action → page-1 reload without loading spinner. (8) Dismiss — swipe Dismiss when !showDismissed → row removed optimistically; on API failure → row reinserted at original index. (9) Restore — swipe Restore when showDismissed → row removed optimistically; on failure → reinserted. (10) Dismiss not shown when showDismissed=true; Restore not shown when showDismissed=false. (11) Navigation — tap row → ThreadPagerView(threads:startIndex:) with correct index. (12) Error state — getThreads throws → error message + Retry button shown.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: Stale-index risk on swipe actions (documented in risks). Next-page errors are silently swallowed — no user-visible feedback if pagination fails mid-scroll. Task cancellation not implemented for concurrent sort/filter changes — last write wins, which is correct but may result in a brief flash of stale data if two changes fire in quick succession.

conflict_risks: ThreadPagerView and ThreadCardView are read-only dependencies; no conflict risk. APIClient.getThreads, dismissThread, restoreThread used as-is.

execution_history.summary: Replaced the ThreadsView stub with a full paginated, filterable thread list. The view covers all acceptance criteria: not-configured guard, loading/loaded/error phases, first-page fetch on appear, sort and showDismissed change triggers, infinite scroll with concurrent-fetch guard, pull-to-refresh (keeps list visible), dismiss/restore swipe actions with optimistic removal and rollback, navigation to ThreadPagerView, and Liquid Glass treatment matching SourcesView. Build verified: BUILD SUCCEEDED.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

## Corrective bead scope

If your `bead_id` ends in `-corrective` or `-corrective-<N>` (e.g. `B-abc12345-corrective`, `B-abc12345-test-corrective-2`), you are running as a CORRECTIVE bead. Your scope is **strictly limited to fixing the specific failure that blocked the parent bead**. Do not:

- Add unrelated improvements, refactors, or cleanup, even if you notice them
- Reapply previously-reverted changes (the operator reverted them for a reason)
- Expand the surface area beyond what is needed to unblock the parent
- Touch files outside what the parent bead's `expected_files` / `expected_globs` covered, unless the failure is genuinely in another file and there is no smaller fix

If you notice an unrelated issue while fixing the corrective scope: **file a separate bead** via `takt bead create` describing what you saw, but do not fix it in this corrective. The operator will prioritize and dispatch.

When in doubt about scope: prefer the smallest possible fix. The reviewer of this corrective will check that the scope was respected.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `Swift` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: ``.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

## Memory

**Read memory at bead start.** Before touching any code, run three searches using `$TAKT_CMD` (injected by the orchestrator):

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace global
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace specs
```

Treat results as ambient context — apply relevant entries; skip entries that don't apply.

**Write to memory when you discover reusable project knowledge** — something that would have changed your approach if you had known it upfront, and is not already in CLAUDE.md or the guardrails.

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory add "<concise fact>" --namespace global               # project-wide knowledge
$TAKT_CMD memory add "<discovery>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>  # feature-scoped
```

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
